title: trichomanes acrostichoides
intro: Trichomanes acrostichoides, also known as the parachute fern or hanging fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to North America and is known for its delicate, lacy fronds that resemble a parachute. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Trichomanes acrostichoides include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health, with herbal preparations often involving teas or infusions made from the dried fronds.
